rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROBIN WILLIAMS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR APPOINTMENT OF SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/8/25. Examiner's Notes emailed to counsel regarding the following issues: 1) Direct addresses of beneficiaries and the trustee were not listed, and they have not been given direct notice. 2) Petitioner requests that Mechanics Bank be appointed as successor trustee after Arnold Kassoy; however, there is currently no vacancy, and this appears to be a request to amend the terms of the trust (which would require an amendment to the petition, Probate Code citation, and notice thereon). 3) Petitioner requests that the trustee be ordered to execute a signed writing designating Mechanics Bank to serve as successor trustee; however, the prior order was based upon stipulation of the parties. The trustee's declaration filed on 12/3/24 only consents to Mechanics Bank serving as successor trustee. Please file a declaration by the trustee specifically consenting to "executing a signed writing designating Mechanics Bank to serve alone as successor Trustee of the Susan Williams Marital Trust in the event that [he] becomes unable or unwilling to serve as Trustee of the Susan Williams Marital Trust".

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TEDMAN MARK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/19/2025 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. The caption must begin with "Order for Final Distribution" as required by LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(b). 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) Paragraph 1 of the petition: i) No Exhibit A was attached. ii) Notice must be given to the petitioner's former attorney of record (John Palley) absent a waiver. See CRC 7.704(b). 4) Paragraph 15: The election was not timely filed (within 4 months after letters were issued). No court approval has been obtained or sought for a late election under Probate Code section 13502(b). 5) Paragraph 20: Liabilities include encumbrances and liens on the estate's real property. See section 1063(g). 6) Paragraph 22: The statutory fees must be apportioned between the petitioner's former and current attorneys of record. If the former attorney waived or agreed to receive a specific sum, please file a signed waiver or agreement. If there is no waiver or agreement, the statutory fees must be apportioned according to the services actually rendered by each attorney. See section 10814 & CRC 7.704(a). 7) Paragraph 23: i) No declaration required by CRC 7.702 is on file. ii) Costs sought to be reimbursed to the attorney must also be itemized. See CRC 7.550(b)(7). iii) Please provide the attorney's time records for ordinary legal services. 8) Paragraph 24: Costs sought to be reimbursed to the petitioner must be itemized. See CC 7.550(b)(7). 9) Paragraph 30: i) No tangible personal assets on hand (BMW, gold chains, etc.) have been inventoried and appraised. ii) Hyatt Highland time share property has not been inventoried and appraised. iii) How should the cash on hand ($244,748) be applied and allocated among the expenses of administration sought to be approved (reserve, petitioner's fees and costs, former/current attorneys' fees and costs, guardian ad litem's fees and costs)? 10) If the guardian ad litem for Jameson Mark intends to request fees and costs, his petition requesting the Court's approval must be on file. The payment source must also be specified. See section 1003(c). 11) Has the guardian ad item for Jameson Mark consented to the waiver of account and assignment of property signed by Jameson Mark?

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF RACHEL MARY KAVANAGH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues to be addressed at hearing: 1) No notice of hearing has been filed and the former minor does not appear to have waived notice. 2) Waivers of final accounts are not favored and, if sought, the Court will require the attorney and the guardian to appear at the hearing. 3) Attorney's fees are requested in the body of the petition but not in the prayer. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FELICIE AGUERRE FREITAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner alleges in the petition that the sale proceeds of $425,929 were deposited into an interest-bearing "sweep account" at Pacific Western; however, the bank statement filed shows that it was deposited into Banc of California into what appears to be a regular interest-bearing checking account, which would not be fully FDIC insured. 2) The accounting ends on 10/11/24, but the bank statement filed to verify the cash covers the period of 10/15/24 to 11/11/24. 3) Petitioner requests extraordinary fees, but he has only provided 17.9 hours of ordinary services, and statutory fees appear to satisfy time for all services provided. 4) Petitioner requests extraordinary fees for services provided to remove the administrator, which was performed prior to his appointment. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MOHAMMAD AMIN YOUSEF
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/19/25. Examiner's Notes were sent to Petitioner on 2/13/25 with the following issues: 1)The caption of the petition and Item 2 are incomplete. It is unknown what Petitioner is requesting. 2)Item 2d is incomplete (bond). 3)Item 5a is incomplete. 4)Item 5b is incomplete. 5)Item 6e is marked, but Petitioner alleges that Petitioner is the surviving spouse. 6)Petitioner is not listed in Item 8. 7)A Suad Yousef is listed in Item 8 without identifying relationship to Decedent. 8)Phone number is missing from the Duties and Liabilities and must be re-filed. 9)Notice filed on 1/2/25: a.Item 2 of the Notice is incomplete (person filing the petition) and incorrect (the county is listed as the petitioner). b.Item 3 of the Notice is incomplete. c.No one was provided notice, and it is unknown who should be served notice (due to the petition being incomplete). 10)Proposed order must be re-submitted, which must be completed with the assumption that the petition will be granted as requested.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JANIE KATHLEEN LINDSAY BRAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/11/2025 (no earlier date available). The proof of publication on file does not show the petitioner's request for IAEA authority was published. New notice must be published if the petitioner continues to seek IAEA authority (see item 2c of the petition requesting full authority). In addition, the following issues (e-mailed to the petitioner on 01/06/2025) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-147 form is on file. 3) No DE-147S form is on file. A pleading cover sheet must be attached to this form. 4) No DE-121 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 5) Item 2d(2) of the petition: The bond amount must be alleged. For full IAEA authority, the amount must match the amount alleged at item 3d(7). 6) Item 3a: Was the decedent domiciled in San Francisco County, despite her place of death in Burlingame, San Mateo County? 7) Item 3d(5): Is any real property subject to encumbrances (e.g., mortgage, line of credit, other lien)? If so, the total balance must be alleged. 8) Item 5a(3) or (4) (at least one box) must be checked. 9) Item 5a(7) or (8) (at least one box) must be checked.
